module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode.' Not confidence-as-performance; competence-as-posture.
trace_note: Regenerated 2026-05-15 (file SHA e545ed2b vs prior f62822e9). Change confined to optional lookup link in ## Prework (commit 0fafbbe: scheduled-agents.md merged into claude-code-for-engineers.md § 9). No teaching beat affected. Single-phase module shell; per-phase SHA equals file SHA.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — practitioner fluency is M6's contracted end-state; does not pre-empt M7 (awe + team-scale ownership).
rules_evaluated.evidence: ## Next and Human close bracket the module without resolving M7's team-deliberation beat. The 'agents-that-build-agents' lecture names the team-scale extension without landing it; M7 is the landing.

Module frames gaps as data the pattern didn't anticipate, not proof the pattern failed. Key Concepts bullet 1: 'not proof the pattern failed.' Accept-loss blockquote: 'Despair not.' No punitive framing found.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea leads with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea names three positive disciplines: name the gap, route to its home, author the skill. No failure-mode title.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — file matches M6 arc in strategy doc.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M6 section: 'Two runs in the pocket now... Encoding is the third phase of test → learn → encode.' File Big Idea matches verbatim spirit. Lecture sequence (story opener, frontier, closer lectures) matches strategy doc M6 detail. Second skill at ~/.claude/skills/ matches. Evals vocabulary landing at M6 matches strategy: 'M6 is where the word earns its full weight.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bullet 1 → theme #4 (artefact rules, grain-of-salt on self-report). Bullet 2 → theme #2 (compounding; gaps teach the next turn). Bullet 3 → theme #1 (90% correct; evals are the catch infrastructure). Bullet 4 → theme #2 (second-skill IS the loop; encoding). Bullet 5 → theme #2 (loop closes when lesson ships). All five bullets instance at least one recurring AE101 theme.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — §§ 3/4/5 gates run first; breadcrumbs on survivors.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All bullets pass §3 (discipline-lead, not failure-mode). No bullet front-runs M7 (§4 clear). All bullet-theme instances confirmed (§5 above). Breadcrumbs on bullets 1 and 5 use theme-essence hooks in engineer register ('the artefact rules, self-reports don't'; 'the loop closes when the lesson ships'). No standalone 'Themes:' section. §§3/4/5 ran before breadcrumb assessment.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn shape — capability register, strategic theme verbs lead.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bullets: Diagnose / Route / Cut / Author / Map / Name. All verbs lead; no Bloom-tag parentheticals; no mechanism tails. 'Cut' and 'Author' are engineer-register verbs for the discipline. 'Map evals across three surfaces' names the artifact (verifier / judge / gate). 'Name one key learning' in closing Debrief is a concrete engineer-register action.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'share with your neighbor', 'tell the room', 'compare with the person next to you', or authored pause beats found in body. Debrief round describes a format but does not author the conversation — self-study variant explicitly hands off to Teacher Claude for the articulation in conversation. Trainer and group 'choose the exact form' is a maintainer note, not body prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module shell has no fenced prompt blocks. Forcing functions are delegated to the linked exercise file (spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md). Module body frames problem-state, mood, and takeaways only — no body-prose forcing-function language present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block Leap test (lines 78-81): (1) second skill at ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md shaped to one of three forms, invoked on a similar task; (2) CLAUDE.local.md shows at least one rule deleted with commit visible in git history; (3) one-page arc retrospective written from M1–M6 artefacts, not training memory. Arc-mood carve-out applied (names artefact + use, not activity).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure modes + escape hatches present for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block Failure modes (lines 92-99): seven rows covering Phase 1 both-runs collapse, Phase 1 addition-only compounding, Phase 2 skill-shape mismatch, Phase 2 author-in-editor-not-conversation, Phase 2 rubber-stamp self-audit, Phase 2 skill-as-documentation, Arc retrospective as trainer-monologue. Each row names failure mode + one escape hatch.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module structural-failure rescue callouts at module open when prior module send-off can fail.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 25-27 (## Start here): two consecutive blockquotes — rescue gate ('nudge / re-send fresh / skip opener') and accept-loss floor ('you can still do this module's exercises. Despair not.'). Maintainer block line 71 documents the deliberate rule-5 exception for the golden-path-only carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; ceiling-showing or arming lectures can open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opener lectures (the-2-frontiers, story-of-module-6) are ceiling-showing and permission-giving respectively — correctly placed before exercise. Closing lectures (the-loop-has-a-name, agents-that-build-agents) are meta-frame / pattern-naming — correctly placed after exercise in ## Human close. Strategy doc rationale: 'Goes first because permission works before the work, not after.'

Rule 2 targets contrast-mood modules (M5-style). M6's contracted mood is practitioner fluency/leverage — not a contrast-mood module. Two opener lectures before the main exercise is appropriate dosage for this mood. Neither opener teaches named practitioner frameworks or tradeoffs-with-reasons; both arm (ceiling + permission).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — two-run gap diagnosis + second-skill authoring reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment is carried primarily by the linked exercise file. Module shell sets problem-state ('You walk in holding two runs of the same task') and delivers vocabulary frame (Key Concepts). A student with both runs in hand enters the exercise with correct problem-posture. Rescue blockquotes cover students with thin or missing M5 run. Under-scaffolded phases: none. Trace phase 1 confusion flag (evals vocab slightly ahead) is resolved by closing lectures — placement correct by design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module matches strategy doc M6 Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File Big Idea: 'Two runs of the same task... are enough evidence to name what the three-pattern didn't anticipate, pick where the gap belongs (memory, verifier, new skill), and author a session-shaper skill.' Strategy doc M6 spirit: 'test → learn → encode... Author the second skill — the learning loop packaged.' All three moves (name / route / encode) present in both.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — close hands off correctly to next module open or real-world practice.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ## Next (line 66): 'The next task you start, package it and send it off, then read what comes back through the lens you built. The reading is the loop, not the sending. It's yours now.' M6 is the final core module; bridge is to real-world practice, not to M7 (optional). agents-that-build-agents lecture carries the M7 entry-point for cohorts running the full arc. Strategy doc M6 → M7 hand-off: 'practitioner fluency is what makes M7's deliberation feel earned.'
